skill.name: maxhub-linkedin
skill.description: LinkedIn professional data assistant that queries user profiles, company information, job listings, posts, comments, and ads. Supports both V1 and V2 API versions through the MaxHub API service.
skill.summary: Query LinkedIn professional data including profiles, companies, jobs, and content via the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: All 855 static findings are false positives - they originate from markdown documentation and reference files showing curl command examples, not from executable code. The skill is a legitimate API wrapper that uses curl for HTTP requests to the MaxHub API service. The legitimate risk factors (external_commands, network, env_access) are inherent to its function as a data query assistant and are clearly documented. No malicious intent or obfuscation was found.

medium_findings.title: Shell command execution via curl
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to execute curl commands for API calls. While this is legitimate functionality for an API wrapper, it runs shell commands with network access and an API key. All commands are templated with documented endpoints and do not accept arbitrary user input for the command structure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Curl commands are documented and templated, but shell execution with environment variable access always carries inherent risk.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API key
medium_findings.description: The skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from environment variables and uses it as a Bearer token for API authentication. This is a standard and necessary pattern for API wrapper skills.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key access is documented and required for the skill to function. The skill explicitly instructs keeping API keys out of outputs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Markdown documentation contains hardcoded URLs to the MaxHub API service at www.aconfig.cn. These are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to the documented API provider. No suspicious or unknown domains detected.

low_findings.title: Data query patterns in reference documentation
low_findings.description: Reference files document endpoints for querying LinkedIn user profiles, company data, and job listings. This is the intended functionality of the skill, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference files document the API endpoints that the skill calls. Data lookup is the declared purpose of the skill.

content.user_title: Query LinkedIn professional data with AI assistance
content.value_statement: LinkedIn data is locked inside a closed platform with no official public API for most use cases. This skill unlocks professional profile data, company information, job listings, and content analytics through the MaxHub API, enabling AI-powered business intelligence, recruitment analysis, and market research.
actual_capabilities: Query LinkedIn user profiles including experience, education, skills, certifications, and publications
Retrieve company information including employee counts, job listings, locations, and affiliated pages
Search job listings and people across LinkedIn with filtering parameters
Fetch post details, comments, reactions, and reposts for content analysis
Support Chinese and English language responses with localized number formatting
limitations: Data quality depends on the third-party MaxHub API, not official LinkedIn APIs
Some API endpoints may return 403 errors for restricted data
Cannot perform actions on LinkedIn such as posting, messaging, or connection requests
Rate limits apply and may throttle frequent requests

use_cases.title: B2B lead research and company profiling
use_cases.description: Sales professionals can research target companies by querying employee counts, key personnel, job openings, and recent company posts to prepare for outreach.

use_cases.title: Talent market analysis for recruiters
use_cases.description: Recruiters and HR teams can analyze job market trends, identify companies hiring for specific roles, and understand skill requirements across industries.

use_cases.title: Competitive intelligence monitoring
use_cases.description: Strategy teams can monitor competitor companies by tracking their employee changes, job posting patterns, and content activity over time.
use_cases.target_user: Business strategy and market research analysts

prompt_templates.title: Research a company profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Get me detailed information about [company name] on LinkedIn. I want to see their company profile, employee count, recent job openings, and locations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick company research for sales or partnership outreach

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a LinkedIn user profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the LinkedIn profile of [person name]. Get their work experience, education, skills, and certifications. Summarize their career trajectory and key expertise areas.

prompt_templates.title: Search for job market trends
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [job title] positions in [location]. Show me which companies are hiring the most, what skills they require, and the seniority levels available.
prompt_templates.scenario: Market research for recruiters or job seekers

prompt_templates.title: Deep competitive analysis with data synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Do a deep analysis of [company name]. Get their company profile, employee breakdown, recent posts, job listings, and similar companies. Combine all this data into a comprehensive competitive intelligence report with key findings and recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Strategic competitive analysis requiring multi-endpoint data synthesis

output_examples.input: Get me information about Acme Corp on LinkedIn
output_examples.output:
## Acme Corp - Company Profile

**Industry:** Enterprise Software
**Company Size:** 1,001-5,000 employees
**Headquarters:** San Francisco, CA

### Recent Job Openings (5)
| Role | Location | Level |
|------|----------|-------|
| Senior Software Engineer | Remote | Senior |
| Product Manager | San Francisco | Mid-Senior |
| Sales Director | New York | Director |

### Key Insights
- Hiring has increased 25% compared to last quarter
- Engineering roles make up 40% of open positions
- Company recently expanded to 3 new markets

output_examples.input: Compare Microsoft, Google, and Apple engineering hiring
output_examples.output:
## Engineering Hiring Comparison

| Metric | Microsoft | Google | Apple |
|--------|-----------|--------|-------|
| Open Engineering Roles | 1,200+ | 900+ | 750+ |
| Top Skill | Azure/AI | ML/AI | Hardware |
| Remote % | 35% | 25% | 15% |

### Key Findings
- All three prioritize AI/ML engineering roles
- Microsoft has the most aggressive hiring ramp
- Apple maintains the lowest remote-work ratio

### Recommendation
Microsoft is the best target for remote AI engineering roles. Apple requires relocation for most positions.

best_practices: Start with a simple single-endpoint query to verify API access, then progress to multi-endpoint deep dives for comprehensive analysis
Use parallel execution for independent data queries to minimize response time when building a company or user profile
Keep API key values out of all output text and error messages to prevent credential exposure
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to scrape or bulk download all data from an endpoint - this violates API terms and may trigger rate limiting
Do not make sequential API calls when queries are independent - always parallelize to reduce latency
Do not expose raw API responses directly to users - format and summarize data for readability

faq.question: Do I need my own API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You must register at https://www.aconfig.cn and create an API key. Set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is this an official LinkedIn API integration?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses the MaxHub API service which provides access to LinkedIn data through its own API endpoints.

faq.question: Can this skill post messages or connect with people on LinkedIn?
faq.answer: No. The skill is read-only. It can query profiles, companies, jobs, and posts but cannot perform any write actions on LinkedIn.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both Chinese and English. It detects your language and responds in the same language with localized number formatting.

faq.question: How accurate is the data from this skill?
faq.answer: Data comes from a third-party API and is for reference only. Some fields may be incomplete or outdated. Always verify critical information.

faq.question: What happens if an API call fails?
faq.answer: The skill handles common errors: 401 means your API key is invalid, 403 means insufficient permissions, 404 means no data found, 429 means rate limited.
